title: A Deep Dive into SoFi's Loan Platform Business
text: SoFi's loan platform business has emerged as a critical growth driver, providing a capital-light volume channel that expands the origination ceiling. The loan platform allows SoFi to originate loans on behalf of third parties, supplementing balance sheet lending. In Q1 2026, the loan platform added $3.6 billion in new partner commitments, with demand from partners exceeding contractual obligations. This represents a significant expansion opportunity for the company. The platform generated $3.7 billion in originations on behalf of third parties in Q4 2025, demonstrating strong partner demand. This business model enables SoFi to scale loan originations without proportional increases in balance sheet funding requirements, improving capital efficiency. The technology and banking-as-a-service segment, which includes the loan platform, declined 27% year-over-year in Q1 2026 due to a large customer departure, but management expects recovery from this one-time event. The loan platform represents a key strategic differentiator as SoFi transitions from a lending-focused company to a broader fintech platform.
